header.name: Nathan Cheng
subtitle: Designer who codes, with a background in film studies and graphic design.

sections.label: Skills
bullets: **Development:** JavaScript/TypeScript, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Astro, Bun, Supabase, Framer Motion, Git, Cloudflare (Workers, R2, Zero Trust), MCP and API integrations, agent skills, MCP server development, agent context engineering
**Design & UX:** Interface design, Design systems, Information architecture, Visual design, Typography, CSS animation, Motion graphics, Accessibility (ARIA, keyboard handling)
**Tools & Platforms:** Claude Code, Cursor, Figma, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, Mintlify, Obsidian, Portainer, Synology DSM

entries.title: Personal portfolio site
bullets: **Designed and built a personal portfolio site** with filtered views by medium and category, shareable via URL state.

**Built motion details across the site:** scroll-driven card stacking from iOS notification physics, shader-driven hero, morphing transitions with spring physics, layout-animated selection indicators.

**Consolidated content + media to MDX in repo + Cloudflare R2** (was Sanity CMS + Cloudinary) — simpler stack and billing, lower maintenance burden, content edits ship with code, agents read and edit directly.

entries.title: Personal knowledge infrastructure
bullets: **Public library of 30+ agent skills (~1K weekly installs) on Vercel's registry** — modular workflows for knowledge maintenance, code review, and project shaping.
**Maintain a 5,000+ note Obsidian vault** as a personal/work context store — long-running notes power my Claude Code workflows and bridge context across my GitHub repos.

**Published `obsidian-remote-mcp`,** open-source server exposing a vault to remote AI clients with OAuth + scoped access — agents pull an outline, read a section, or update a property without dumping whole files.

**Built and operate a Synology-based personal server stack** hosting the vault, a persistent Claude Code agent, `obsidian-remote-mcp`, and supporting services; Tailscale tailnet + Cloudflare Zero Trust SSO.

entries.title: Design and Systems Manager
entries.summary: Solo designer / systems manager at family-run B2B software company (users at NASA, Eli Lilly, 3M, EPA, Nestle).
bullets: **Built a Next.js + Supabase replacement for a 25-year-old Microsoft Access customer database (40,000+ records).** Solo, ~70 commits over six weeks; outside firms had quoted $25–60k for a from-scratch rebuild.

**Replatformed customer support docs off WordPress onto Mintlify** via a Firecrawl + Bun + Python pipeline — migrated 219 articles into ~224 MDX files, with a Claude Haiku agent handling repetitive cleanup; built for AI-crawler legibility.
**Designed workflow builder interface unifying 40+ configuration options:** Windows UI patterns, component system with progressive disclosure for serial device communication.
**Reduced recurring setup questions ~30%** with a walkthrough video series replacing 90s-era documentation — scripted, recorded, edited with motion graphics.

entries.title: Designer and Developer
bullets: **Built React/Next.js config generator for LLM interview chatbot,** cutting setup errors for screening flows.
**Led pitch deck and narrative strategy for 3 Techstars startups** preparing for Demo Day — competitor analysis, problem-focused storytelling, visual direction.

entries.title: Fellowship at Venture for America (now Ember Fellowship)
bullets: **Selected for competitive entrepreneurship fellowship (10% acceptance)** — IDEO, Frog Design, and McKinsey workshops on product strategy and design thinking.
**Led 5-person team to win Judge Pick and Crowd Favorite in 3-day product sprint:** defined MVP scope, designed UI prototypes, pitched city exploration app to VC/executive panel.

entries.title: Web development foundations (The Odin Project) + Animations on the Web (Emil Kowalski)
Wesleyan University - B.A. in Film Studies, Minor in Integrated Design and Engineering
bullets: **3.71 GPA;** Honors, Dean's List; Frank Capra Prize for excellence in cinematic filmmaking, humor, and pathos

**Led team to first place in game development intensive led by Bethesda founder:** Managed 4-person team from concept to iOS demo, planned levels and art direction, worked in Unity game engine, pitched to industry jury (AFI, Warner Bros. Games)
